title: Hardy Hibiscus
description: This particular Hardy Hibiscus can grow over 10 feet tall, and it can make a good wall of foliage and flowers.

title: Dahlia
description: The Dahlia is a cut-flower that we started growing after being inspired by Floret Flower Farm.

title: Rudbeckia
description: Beyond providing sturdy and durable cut-flowers, Rudbeckia will come back each year, and they grow great alongside Echinacea.

title: Marigold
description: Marigolds are one of the most important flowers in my garden due to the fact that they offer pest resistance to my tomatoes by repelling whiteflies and reducing harmful soil nematodes. They release natural chemicals that confuse pests and destroy nematode eggs. All while bringing in pollinators!

title: Peach Melba Nasturtium
description: Peach Melba's blooms can go in your salad, and its seeds can replace pepper. I call this one "the chef's nasturtium."

title: Lily (Pink)
description: While not all 'lilies' are true lilies, please note that true lilies are extremely toxic to cats. Always think about the pets while designing your garden.

title: Dwarf Jewel Nasturtium
description: The Dwarf Jewel Nasturtium did not perform very well in my zone (a very humid 8a), but I have seen that they work great in San Diego. I've had way too much daily rain, so perhaps that was the problem. I will try again next year.

title: Lily (Dark Red)
description: Lilies go back thousands of years, with the Madonna being one of the oldest known cultivars of any flower. Did you know Egyptians made famous, ancient perfumes with lilies, myrrh, and cinnamon?

title: Lily (Yellow and Peach)
description: Humans and lilies have a long, symbolic history together: Lilies are the traditional birth flower for May and the classic flower to celebrate a 30th wedding anniversary.

title: The Petunias Self-Seeded
description: Petunias are beautiful flowers that thrive. One of them even grew from a crack in our concrete.

title: Tropical Hibiscus
description: Tropical Hibiscus is my favorite. They offer very durable and extraordinarily vibrant blooms that work in salads and teas.

title: Last Year's Sunflower
description: This is a sunflower from last season.

title: Seedlings
description: Seedlings! I have almost 50 Bell Pepper plants, 16 squash, 16 tomatoes, and a bunch of rudbeckia.

title: Tiny Seedling
alt: First seedling of the season this year
description: First seedling of the season this year.
